details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-utensils]] Head to **Huen Phen** in the Old City.
At night, the inner rooms open up to reveal an eccentric, atmospheric dining hall packed with Lanna antiques. Order traditional Northern dishes like Sai Oua (sausage) and Gaeng Hang Lay (curry).

schedule_items.title: North Gate Jazz Co-Op 🎷
details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-music]] End your birthday night at the Chang Phuak Gate.
Join the bohemian crowd spilling out onto the street, grab a cheap drink, and listen to world-class live jazz. A legendary, high-energy Chiang Mai experience.

overview_markdown: A full day immersed in extreme nature: exploring Thailand's highest peak and ethically connecting with rescued elephants, followed by a dive into Chiang Mai's sprawling night markets.
context_markdown:
### Doi Inthanon: The Roof of Thailand
Doi Inthanon is the highest mountain in Thailand (2,565 meters). It is part of the Himalayan mountain range, offering a completely different climate and ecosystem compared to the rest of the country. The national park is famous for its massive, powerful waterfalls (like Wachirathan), misty cloud forests, and the striking Twin Royal Pagodas built by the Thai Air Force to honor the King and Queen.

### Ethical Elephant Sanctuaries
The relationship between humans and elephants in Thailand is historically complex, shifting from logging and warfare to tourism. Today, the focus is on ethical sanctuaries that rescue and rehabilitate elephants from abusive riding camps. In ethical camps (often run by or partnering with local Karen hill tribes), visitors do not ride the animals. Instead, you enter their world: feeding them, walking with them through the jungle, and bathing with them in the river.

### The Night Bazaar and Chiang Mai Nightlife
The Chiang Mai Night Bazaar on Chang Klan Road is one of the oldest and most famous night markets in Thailand. It is a sprawling, chaotic maze of stalls selling handicrafts, clothing, and souvenirs. Within this area, spaces like the Ploen Ruedee Night Market offer a more curated, festive food-court experience with live music. For those seeking late-night clubbing and backpacker energy, the Zoe in Yellow complex back in the Old City is the undisputed epicenter of nightlife.

schedule_items.title: Meeting for the Elephant & Mountain Tour 🐘
details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-map-pin]] Meet at the **Pon Elephant Thailand** office on Kotchasarn Road (unless you arranged hotel pickup).

schedule_items.title: Doi Inthanon National Park 🏔️
details: Drive southwest to the highest mountain in Thailand.
Stop to feel the mist at the massive 80-meter **Wachirathan Waterfall**.
[[ICON:fa-solid fa-camera]] Visit the stunning **Twin Royal Pagodas** surrounded by terraced flower gardens and panoramic views.
Enjoy an included local lunch.

schedule_items.title: Ethical Elephant Encounters 🍌
details: Arrive at the Karen hill-tribe village and change into traditional shirts.
Feed the elephants fruits and hike with them through the lush jungle.
[[ICON:fa-solid fa-water]] Swim and bathe with the elephants at a hidden waterfall.
Take a quick shower at the camp to wash off the jungle mud before departing.

schedule_items.title: Return & Refresh 🚿
details: Arrive back in Chiang Mai. Head to the hotel for a proper shower and to change for the evening.

schedule_items.title: Northern Dinner (Nimman or Chang Phueak) 🍲
details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-utensils]] If in the area and still open, grab a bowl of famous Michelin-recognized curry noodles at **Khao Soi Mae Sai**.
Alternative: **Tong Tem Toh** in Nimman for an excellent, buzzing local Northern Thai dinner.

schedule_items.title: Chiang Mai Night Bazaar Chaos 🛍️
details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-person-walking]] Head east to Chang Klan Road to explore the sprawling **Chiang Mai Night Bazaar**.
[[ICON:fa-solid fa-utensils]] For food and drinks in a great atmosphere, step into the **Ploen Ruedee Night Market** courtyard (live music, hay bales, and clean street food).
[[ICON:fa-solid fa-martini-glass-citrus]] If you want intense backpacker clubbing/party vibes to end the night, head back into the Old City to the **Zoe In Yellow** complex.

context_markdown:
## [[ICON:fa-solid fa-water]] The Andaman Coast: Phuket & Krabi
The southern peninsula of Thailand is defined by the Andaman Sea. This region is world-famous for its dramatic limestone karsts jutting out of emerald waters, dense jungles, and vibrant beach towns. 

### Phuket: The Pearl of the Andaman
Phuket is Thailand's largest island, connected to the mainland by a bridge. It is a place of extremes: you can find untouched, pristine hidden coves right next to some of the most intense and chaotic nightlife streets in the world. Patong is the epicenter of this energy, offering a raw, unfiltered coastal party town experience.

### The Longtail Boat Culture
The traditional Thai longtail boat (Ruea Hang Yao) is the icon of the southern islands. Originally designed for river transport, the design was adapted for the sea by mounting a massive automotive engine on the back, with the propeller attached to a long pole. They are loud, colorful, and the most authentic way to reach isolated beaches where no roads go.

overview_markdown: A perfect transition day. Leave the northern mountains behind, fly to Thailand's biggest island, escape to a hidden tropical cove by longtail boat, and dive headfirst into the neon chaos of Patong's nightlife.
context_markdown:
### Freedom Beach: The Hidden Paradise
Just a few kilometers south of the frantic energy of Patong lies Freedom Beach, often cited as one of the most beautiful strips of white sand in Phuket. Because it is surrounded by steep, jungle-covered hills and private land, there is no easy road access. The best way to reach it is by hiring a traditional longtail boat from the southern end of Patong Beach. Arriving mid-afternoon is a strategic move: the day-trippers are leaving, the sun is less intense, and you get the pristine water largely to yourself.

### Banzaan Fresh Market
Located behind the massive Jungceylon shopping mall, Banzaan is a modern take on the traditional Thai fresh market. The ground floor is a bustling wet market selling exotic fruits, vegetables, and incredibly fresh seafood directly from the Andaman Sea. The true experience is buying raw tiger prawns, crab, or fish downstairs, and taking it to the upstairs food court where local chefs will cook it to your exact specifications for a small fee.

### Bangla Road (Soi Bangla)
Bangla Road is the undisputed heart of Phuket's nightlife. After sunset, the road is closed to traffic and transforms into a chaotic, neon-drenched sensory overload. It is lined with massive open-air beer bars, live music venues, towering nightclubs (like Illuzion), ping-pong show touts, and street performers. It is loud, unapologetic, and an absolute must-see spectacle, even if you just grab a beer and watch the human circus go by.

schedule_items.title: To Chiang Mai Airport 🚕
details: Enjoy a slow morning and a good coffee. No need to rush.
[[ICON:fa-solid fa-taxi]] Take a Grab to Chiang Mai Airport (CNX). It's a short 10-15 minute ride, but allow some buffer for morning traffic.

schedule_items.title: Flight to Phuket ✈️
details: **Thai Vietjet Flight VZ414**. Grab a window seat to catch views of the limestone islands as you descend into the south.

schedule_items.title: Arrival & Transfer to Patong 🚐
details: Land at HKT, grab your bags. The airport will be busy.
Ignore the touts and use the official metered taxi stands or Grab/Bolt to head to Patong (about a 1-hour drive).
[[ICON:fa-solid fa-suitcase]] Check into your hotel, drop the bags, and immediately put on your swimwear.

schedule_items.title: Longtail to Freedom Beach 🚤🏖️
details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-ship]] Walk down to Patong Beach and negotiate a private longtail boat to **Freedom Beach** (expect around 1500-2000 THB round trip).
A scenic 10-15 minute ride takes you to a pristine, roadless cove with white sand and clear water.
[[ICON:fa-solid fa-sun]] Arriving mid-afternoon means you avoid the peak crowds. Agree on a pickup time with your boatman before sunset (around 17:30) as the sea gets rougher.

schedule_items.title: Sunset & Shower 🌅
details: Return to Patong Beach by boat just as the sun sets over the Andaman Sea.
Head back to the hotel for a shower and get ready for the night.

schedule_items.title: Seafood Feast at Banzaan Market 🦞
details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-utensils]] Head to the **Banzaan Fresh Market** (behind Jungceylon).
Buy fresh, raw seafood on the ground floor, then take it upstairs to the food court where they will cook it perfectly to your liking for a small fee.

schedule_items.title: The Chaos of Bangla Road 💥
details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-martini-glass-citrus]] Walk into **Bangla Road**, the undisputed epicenter of Phuket's nightlife.
It's a sensory overload of neon, booming music, and massive open-air bars. Grab a beer, people-watch, or hit one of the mega-clubs like Illuzion to celebrate your arrival in the south.

overview_markdown: A full-day speedboat excursion into the dramatic limestone seascape of Phang Nga Bay. You will explore hidden sea caves by canoe, walk through a floating village, and see the iconic James Bond Island.
context_markdown:
### Phang Nga Bay: A Geological Wonder
Phang Nga Bay is one of the most visually stunning marine landscapes in the world. It is characterized by its sheer limestone karsts that jut vertically out of the emerald-green water. These formations are millions of years old, created by the tectonic shifts and erosion of ancient coral reefs. Geologically, it shares the same origins as Halong Bay in Vietnam and Guilin in China. Navigating through this bay feels prehistoric.

### Koh Panyee: The Floating Village
Koh Panyee is a remarkable Muslim fishing village built entirely on stilts over the water, leaning against a giant limestone cliff. It was established at the end of the 18th century by nomadic Malay fishermen. Because Thai law at the time restricted land ownership to citizens, the settlers built their homes over the sea. Today, it has a mosque, a school, and its famously unique floating football pitch, which was originally built from old scraps of wood and fishing rafts by the village children.

### Khao Phing Kan (James Bond Island)
Before 1974, Khao Phing Kan was a rarely visited, remote island in the bay. That changed completely when it was featured in the James Bond movie *"The Man with the Golden Gun"* starring Roger Moore. The movie immortalized the striking 20-meter-tall limestone needle rock called Ko Ta Pu, which stands isolated in the water just off the beach. Today, it is an iconic (though often crowded) symbol of Thailand's southern islands.

### Sea Canoeing (Hong)
A "Hong" translates to "room" in Thai. These are collapsed cave systems inside the limestone islands that form hidden, open-air lagoons accessible only through narrow sea caves at low tide. Gliding into a hong on a sea canoe reveals a quiet, isolated micro-ecosystem of mangroves, monkeys, and vertical cliff walls.

schedule_items.title: Wake Up & Prep 🎒
details: Wear your swimsuit under your clothes.
Pack a day bag: towel, high-SPF sunscreen, sunglasses, hat, and a change of dry clothes.
Skip the heavy breakfast hunt; a light breakfast is provided at the pier.

schedule_items.title: Hotel Pickup 🚐
details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-van-shuttle]] Wait in the hotel lobby. The shared minivan will pick you up and head across the island to the Boat Lagoon Pier on the east coast.

schedule_items.title: Pier Arrival & Briefing ☕
details: Check-in with the crew.
[[ICON:fa-solid fa-mug-hot]] Enjoy a light complimentary breakfast (coffee, tea, juice, toast, cookies).
Listen to the safety briefing before boarding the speedboat.

schedule_items.title: The 7 Wonders of Phang Nga 🚤
details: Depart by speedboat toward **Koh Yao Yai** for a quick stop at the private Palm Beach Club.
Cruise to **Panak Island** to explore the Ice Cream Cave or Mangrove Cave.
[[ICON:fa-solid fa-ship]] The highlight: arrive at **Hong Island** and board sea canoes (paddled by local guides) to glide through spectacular natural sea caves into hidden lagoons.

schedule_items.title: The Floating Village Lunch 🍛
details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-utensils]] Arrive at **Koh Panyee**, a fascinating stilt village built entirely over the water.
Enjoy an included buffet lunch (Thai, Western, and Halal options).
Take some time to walk the wooden boardwalks and see the famous floating football pitch.

schedule_items.title: The Man with the Golden Gun 🏝️
details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-camera]] Visit **Khao Phing Kan** (James Bond Island) and snap the classic photo of the needle-like Ko Ta Pu rock.
Head to **Naka Island** for the final stop. Relax on the beach or swim in the clear waters before the journey back.

schedule_items.title: Return to Patong 🚿
details: Speedboat returns to Boat Lagoon Pier by 17:00-17:30.
[[ICON:fa-solid fa-van-shuttle]] Transfer back to your hotel in Patong.
Take the greatest shower of your life to wash off the salt and sunscreen.

schedule_items.title: Soft Evening Recovery 🌙
details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-utensils]] 8 hours on a speedboat will leave you with 'sea legs' and heavy fatigue.
Keep dinner simple: eat at a beachside restaurant in Patong or return to Banzaan Market.
Consider getting an aloe vera or foot massage before sleeping.

overview_markdown: A tactical strike on the biggest water festival in the world. Spend the morning in Phuket Old Town for a beautiful, highly photogenic water battle amidst historic mansions and street art, then ride an open-air tuk-tuk straight into the absolute madness of Patong.
context_markdown:
### Songkran: The Thai New Year
Songkran originally is a deeply spiritual Buddhist festival marking the traditional Thai New Year. The core ritual involves pouring scented water over Buddha statues and the hands of elders to symbolize purification and the washing away of bad luck. Over time, in major cities and tourist hubs, this water-throwing tradition has evolved into the world's largest, craziest, and most joyous multi-day street water fight. 

### The Rules of Engagement
On April 13th, if you step outside, you consent to being soaked. Locals will use buckets of ice-cold water, high-powered water guns, and hoses. Another common tradition is having *din sor phong* (a fragrant, cooling white talcum powder paste) smeared on your cheeks as a blessing. The golden rule of Songkran: no matter how cold the water or how shocked you are, you must always smile. Getting angry is culturally unacceptable.

### Old Town vs. Patong: A Tale of Two Battles
There is absolutely a water war in Phuket Old Town, but it is vastly different from Patong. Old Town is more local, energetic but manageable, and incredibly photogenic. The narrow streets mean intense, fun skirmishes, but you can find shelter under the arched walkways. Patong, on the other hand, is a chaotic, non-stop "meat grinder" of foam machines, massive crowds, and pumping club music. Doing Old Town first allows you to warm up before facing Patong's madness.

### What to See in Phuket Old Town
Beyond the water fights, the Old Town is an architectural gem. In the late 19th century, a tin mining boom brought European mining companies and Hokkien Chinese merchants to Phuket. They built the town in a unique "Sino-Portuguese" style:
- **Soi Romanee & Thalang Road:** The most iconic streets, lined with pastel-colored (often bright pink) townhouses and ornate wooden doors.
- **The "Five-Foot Ways":** Arched, covered sidewalks designed to protect pedestrians from sun and rain (which will also protect your camera from water guns!).
- **Street Art:** Look for vibrant murals hidden in the alleys depicting Phuket's cultural heritage.
- **Chinese Shrines:** Ornate, incense-filled temples tucked between the cafes.

### Southern Thai Cuisine
Phuket is a UNESCO Creative City of Gastronomy. Southern Thai food is famously the spiciest in the country, relying heavily on turmeric, shrimp paste, and fresh seafood. Dishes like *Moo Hong* (sweet braised pork belly) and *Gaeng Pu Bai Chaplu* (yellow crab meat curry with betel leaves) are absolute must-tries while exploring the Old Town.

schedule_items.title: Tactical Escape to Old Town 🚕
details: **Crucial Prep:** Put your phone, cash, and hotel key in a waterproof dry-bag pouch around your neck.
[[ICON:fa-solid fa-taxi]] Take a closed, air-conditioned Grab from Patong to Phuket Old Town before the streets lock down for the festival.

schedule_items.title: Sino-Portuguese Exploration & The First Battle 📸💦
details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-person-walking]] Walk along **Thalang Road** and the incredibly photogenic, pastel-pink **Soi Romanee**.
**The Water Fight Begins:** By 10:00 AM, the local water battles will start. It's incredibly fun and the contrast of splashing water against the heritage buildings makes for stunning photos.
Explore the hidden street art murals and ancient Chinese shrines between the water skirmishes.
Use the arched 'five-foot ways' (covered sidewalks) to take cover and protect your camera.
[[ICON:fa-solid fa-mug-hot]] Grab a coffee at *DouBrew* or an artisanal treat at *Torry's Ice Cream* when you need a break.

schedule_items.title: The Southern Lunch 🍛
details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-utensils]] Eat indoors before the afternoon chaos erupts.
Head to **One Chun** (vintage, quirky vibe) or **Tu Kab Khao** (elegant, colonial vibe with a giant lobster on the wall). Both are Michelin Bib Gourmand.
Order the Crab Meat Curry and the Braised Pork Belly (Moo Hong).

schedule_items.title: The Moving Target (Return to Patong) 🛺
details: Buy massive water guns in Old Town.
[[ICON:fa-solid fa-car]] Hire an open-air Tuk-Tuk to drive you back to Patong.
**Warning:** You are now a moving target. Locals waiting by the roadside will throw buckets of ice water directly into the back of your Tuk-Tuk as you pass. You will arrive soaked.

schedule_items.title: The Patong Water War 🔫💦
details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-person-walking]] Step onto Patong Beach Road and **Bangla Road**.
This is maximum chaos. The streets are closed to traffic and packed with thousands of people, pickup trucks loaded with water barrels, foam machines, and booming music.
Embrace the madness, smile at everyone, and fight back.

schedule_items.title: Shower and Shiver 🚿
details: Return to the hotel. You will be freezing from the ice water and covered in white powder.
Take a very long, very hot shower and change into dry clothes.

schedule_items.title: The New Year's Night 🌙
details: [[ICON:fa-solid fa-utensils]] Head out for dinner. *Note:* Even at night, you might still get splashed, so dress lightly.

[[ICON:fa-solid fa-martini-glass-citrus]] Head back to Bangla Road if you have the energy. The water fights slow down slightly, and the massive nightclubs (like Illuzion) host some of the biggest Songkran parties of the year.
